name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 67 Tuesday. **CF INDUSTRIES +2.6% TUE TO $125.89 ON FERTILIZER WAR-BID** — kinetic restart May 4 + Qatar fertilizer-supply concern persisting + Indian RCF tender May 9 = real fertilizer cascade transmission to India. Argus tender data May 4: Egyptian NCIC sold 20,000t DAP at up to $880/t fob (+5-10% over April $805-820/t avg); urea 10,000t at $852/t fob Ain Sokhna; NCIC offering further 30,000t DAP closing May 4. Indian fertilizer producer/importer RCF issued tender to buy phosphate rock and/or DAP/MAP and MOP closing May 9 — Indian government sourcing aggressively, suggesting agriculture-input strategic stockpiling activity. The May 1 urea -14.23% (TradingEconomics) on diplomatic-window pricing has reversed: urea expected to re-rate +5-10% from $585/T baseline on kinetic restart pricing. Wheat/Corn/Sugar all +2.3-3.0% Tue in sympathy. Linde -2.8% Tue (industrial-gas-producer rotation FAILED — see helium node). QatarEnergy downstream urea HALTED structurally; physical supply chain unchanged through 2027. CF Q1 May 6 BINARY TEST UNCHANGED — May 4 kinetic restart elevates equity-fundamentals tilt. Nutrien +0.8% Tue (lagging CF). The equity-vs-physical divergence is now equity catching up: CF +2.6% on Tue while urea repricing back upward. CF cumulative +38.6% 3M — strongest fertilizer-equity arc of crisis.
